steps.title: Set up similar triangles
steps.content: Because $LM$ is parallel to $PQ$, triangles $LRM$ and $QRP$ are similar. Corresponding sides are proportional, so the segment on $LQ$ matches the segment on $MP$ in the same ratio.

steps.title: Find the missing segment
steps.content:
Use $LR=10$, $MR=9$, and $RP=19$.
$\frac{10}{RQ}=\frac{9}{19}$
$9(RQ)=190$
$RQ=\frac{190}{9}$
This gives the missing part of $LQ$, not the full requested length.

steps.title: Add the two segments
steps.content: The full length $LQ$ is the known segment $LR$ plus the missing segment $RQ$. $10+\frac{190}{9}=\frac{90}{9}+\frac{190}{9}=\frac{280}{9}$ This matches choice D, while choice B is only the missing segment before adding $LR$. The answer is D.
